payload.answer: Knowledge graphs store information as labeled nodes (entities) and labeled edges (relationships). They support semantic queries by leveraging the meaning of nodes, edges, and their types. Common representations are RDF triples (subject-predicate-object) and property graphs (nodes/edges with properties). This provides a flexible foundation for integrating diverse data and enabling advanced reasoning over connected information.

entity_resolution.reason: context provided is general informational; no unique entities requiring disambiguation were specified

memory_cards.statement: Knowledge graphs represent information as nodes (entities) and edges (relationships), enabling semantic queries that go beyond keyword matching.
Entities in a knowledge graph often have types (classes) and attributes, and relationships can be labeled to express meaning (e.g., Person has name, is_friend_of Person).
Common graph representations include RDF triples (subject-predicate-object) and property graphs (nodes/edges with properties).

details: Provided a high-level overview of knowledge graphs with basic components and representations.
